title: Colorimetric sulfo-phospho-vanillin (SPV) method for analysis of lipids in mucin
author: Xueying Zhong
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.euybexw
materials: Then add 1.25 ml chloroform and vortex well;

steps.title: Prepare the mucin samples
steps.instruction: Dissolve the mucins in water at a predetermined concentration;

steps.title: For each 1 ml of sample solution, add 3
steps.instruction: For each 1 ml of sample solution, add 3.75 ml 1:2 (v/v) chloroform: methanol and vortex well;

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.25 ml dH2O and vortex well;

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1000 RPM for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1000 RPM for 5 min to give a two-phase system (aqueous top, organic bottom);

steps.title: Recover the bottom phase with a...
steps.instruction: Recover the bottom phase with a Pasteur pipette and store in tubes.

steps.title: Mix cholesterol in solvent at predetermined concentration, for instance 5mg/ml;
steps.instruction: Mix cholesterol in solvent at predetermined concentration, for instance 5mg/ml;

steps.title: Vary volume of the standard sample...
steps.instruction: Vary volume of the standard sample (1 ul, 2 ul, 4 ul, 8 ul, 16 ul and 24 ul)to assign different amount of cholesterol in different tubes.

steps.title: Evaporating solvent in both standard samples and mucin samples
steps.instruction: Evaporating solvent in both standard samples and mucin samples

steps.title: Prepare the sulfo-phosphoric-vanillin acid agen
steps.instruction: Prepare the sulfo-phosphoric-vanillin acid agen: 0.2 mg vanillin per ml 17% phosphoric acid) for color development;

steps.title: Add 50 ul sulfo-phosphoric-vanillin acid agent for color development;
steps.instruction: Add 50 ul sulfo-phosphoric-vanillin acid agent for color development;

steps.title: Measuring absorbance at 540 nm after...
steps.instruction: Measuring absorbance at 540 nm after 5 min of color development.

description: 1. Add 100 ul concentrated sulfuric acid into each tube and incubating at 90 C for 10 min ( on a dry heating bath); 2. Cooling to room temperature and measuring background absorbance at 540nm;
